team_description: A 5-agent web development team that builds a single-page website from a one-line brief: PM captures the brief, dispatches Content + Visual in parallel, then Designer to build, then QA to review. User talks to the PM only; the team coordinates in one shared MessageBus channel. Generic — works for any landing page, marketing site, event page, or product microsite.
team_intro_md:
## Web Development Team — generic 5-agent build crew

You're the producer. You talk to the **Project Manager** (PM). They:

1. Capture your brief in 60 seconds (writes `project_brief.md` to the shared workspace).
2. Dispatch the team in ONE batch on the `web-build-coordination` channel — Content Creator + Visual in parallel (they unblock the Designer), then Web Designer to build, then QA to review.
3. Surface only decisions / blockers / the finished deliverable back to you.

### Default tech baseline (PM may override per brief)
- Single static `index.html` in `./agent_workspace/`
- Tailwind CSS via CDN (no build step)
- Mobile-first, three breakpoints (375 / 768 / 1280)
- One small interactive JS component, vanilla

### Team
- **Project Manager (PM)** — orchestrator + your single entry point. Owns `project_brief.md`. Never writes code.
- **Web Designer** — semantic HTML + Tailwind, mobile-first, accessibility-aware.
- **Content Creator** — researcher + copywriter; verifies facts via web-search; supplies hero/body/CTA/SEO copy.
- **Visual** — art director; generates hero / section / OG-share imagery via image-gen MCP (or hands off image briefs for external generation).
- **QA Reviewer** — Playwright-driven 3-breakpoint screenshots + WCAG 2.1 AA review; reports prioritized BLOCKER/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW.

### How to drive it (1-hour fresh user)

Message the **Project Manager** with anything from a one-liner to a paragraph, e.g.:

> Build a landing page for our AI tool launch next month. Premium feel, technical audience, signup CTA above the fold.

OR:

> Build an event companion site for <event name> on <dates>. Audience is people who can't attend in person.

PM takes it from there. You'll see brief status updates and the final delivery; @-mention any teammate any time for direct tweaks (e.g. `@Web Designer make the hero full-bleed`).

### Prerequisites (one-time setup)
See the bundle's `SETUP.md` for full walkthrough. Minimum:
- **Playwright MCP** (required for self-preview + QA screenshots): `claude mcp add playwright npx @playwright/mcp@latest` — or run in SSE mode on the cloud host
- **Image-gen MCP** (optional — Visual falls back to writing image briefs if absent)
- An LLM provider configured in Settings (the team is heavier on the PM's orchestration; Claude Sonnet 4.6+ recommended)

### Source
Authored by the NarraNexus team. MIT license. Generic web-build crew — works for any static-site project.

agents.name: Project Manager
agents.role: PM + team orchestrator; single user entry point; owns project_brief.md

agents.name: Web Designer
agents.role: Senior frontend engineer / designer; builds the static site

agents.name: Content Creator
agents.role: Researcher + copywriter; verifies facts; supplies hero/body/CTA/SEO copy

agents.name: Visual
agents.role: Art Director; generates hero/section/OG imagery via image-gen MCP (or writes briefs if no MCP)

agents.name: QA Reviewer
agents.role: QA + WCAG 2.1 AA reviewer; Playwright MCP preview + 3-breakpoint screenshots + a11y audit
